titleStmt.title: Letter from the Administrative Area Officer, Royal Hospital Kilmainham, to the Chief Crown Solicitor, 26 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Administrative Area Officer, Royal Hospital Kilmainham

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by the Administrative Area Officer, Royal Hospital Kilmainham, to the chief crown solicitor. Sir Henry Arthur Wynne (1867-1943), was appointed to the vacant position of chief crown solicitor following the death of Sir Malachy Kelly (1851-1916,) after his death in March 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3785

correspAction.persName: Administrative Area Officer, Royal Hospital Kilmainham

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Ref. No. 92.
Subject. Injury to Ptk. Sheehan. A.A.O. Ireland, Royal Hospital, Dublin. 26th March, 1916. The Chief Crown Solicitor, Dublin Castle. Sir, In further reference to yours of 16/3/16 with
regard to Patrick Sheehan, I find that this man is totally
incapacitated from work with a fractured fibula. I have instructed the District Purchasing Officer, Cork
during the period of such incapacity to pay him a sum not
exceeding 50% of his weekly earnings, such weekly payments
not to exceed Â£1. I have also instructed the D.P.O.S. Cork to have
this man re-examined periodically with a view to ascertain-
ing when he will be able to resume work. Yours faithfully, GM Colonel,
Administrative Area Officer.
M.R.
